00001| import { useState, useCallback } from 'react'
00002| import {
00003|   type Abi,
00004|   type Address,
00005|   type ContractFunctionName,
00006|   type ContractFunctionArgs,
00007|   type ContractFunctionReturnType,
00008|   type Log,
00009|   createPublicClient,
00010|   http,
00011|   encodeFunctionData,
00012|   decodeFunctionResult,
00013|   } from 'viem'
00014| import { useWallet } from './useWallet'
00015| import { getChainById, getSimulationRpcUrl } from '@/lib/blockchain/chains'
00016| import { config } from '../config'
00017| 
00018| /**
00019|  * Represents an asset balance change during transaction simulation
00020|  */
00021| export interface AssetChange {
00022|   /** Token information */
00023|   token: {
00024|     /** Token contract address */
00025|     address: Address
00026|     /** Token decimals */
00027|     decimals?: number
00028|     /** Token symbol */
00029|     symbol?: string
00030|   }
00031| 
00032|   /** Balance change details */
00033|   value: {
00034|     /** Change amount (positive or negative) */
00035|     diff: bigint
00036|     /** Balance before transaction */
00037|     pre: bigint
00038|     /** Balance after transaction */
00039|     post: bigint
00040|   }
00041| }
00042| 
00043| /**
00044|  * Parameters for a single contract call to simulate
00045|  */
00046| export interface SimulateWriteCall<
00047|   TAbi extends Abi = Abi,
00048|   TName extends ContractFunctionName<TAbi, 'nonpayable' | 'payable'> = ContractFunctionName<TAbi, 'nonpayable' | 'payable'>
00049| > {
00050|   /** Contract address to call */
00051|   contractAddress: Address
00052|   /** Chain ID where the contract is deployed */
00053|   chainId: number
00054|   /** Contract ABI */
00055|   abi: TAbi
00056|   /** Function name to call */
00057|   functionName: TName
00058|   /** Function arguments */
00059|   args: ContractFunctionArgs<TAbi, 'nonpayable' | 'payable', TName>
00060|   /** ETH value to send (for payable functions) */
00061|   value?: bigint
00062| }
00063| 
00064| /**
00065|  * Result from a single simulated contract call
00066|  */
00067| export interface CallResult<
00068|   TAbi extends Abi = Abi,
00069|   TName extends ContractFunctionName<TAbi, 'nonpayable' | 'payable'> = ContractFunctionName<TAbi, 'nonpayable' | 'payable'>
00070| > {
00071|   /** Return value from the contract function */
00072|   result: ContractFunctionReturnType<TAbi, 'nonpayable' | 'payable', TName> | undefined
00073|   /** Event logs emitted during the call */
00074|   logs: Log[]
00075|   /** Execution status */
00076|   status: 'success' | 'failure'
00077|   /** Gas consumed by the call */
00078|   gasUsed?: bigint
00079|   /** Error details if the call reverted */
00080|   error?: {
00081|     message: string
00082|   }
00083| }
00084| 
00085| export type SimulateOneTransactionResult<
00086|   TAbi extends Abi,
00087|   TName extends ContractFunctionName<TAbi, 'nonpayable' | 'payable'>
00088| > = {
00089|   callResult: CallResult<TAbi, TName>
00090|   assetChanges?: ReadonlyArray<AssetChange>
00091| }
00092| 
00093| export type SimulateTwoTransactionsResult<
00094|   TAbi1 extends Abi,
00095|   TName1 extends ContractFunctionName<TAbi1, 'nonpayable' | 'payable'>,
00096|   TAbi2 extends Abi,
00097|   TName2 extends ContractFunctionName<TAbi2, 'nonpayable' | 'payable'>
00098| > = {
00099|   firstCallResult: CallResult<TAbi1, TName1>
00100|   secondCallResult: CallResult<TAbi2, TName2>
00101|   assetChanges?: ReadonlyArray<AssetChange>
00102| }
00103| 
00104| export interface UseSimulateWriteContractsReturn {
00105|   simulateOneTransaction: <
00106|     TAbi extends Abi,
00107|     TName extends ContractFunctionName<TAbi, 'nonpayable' | 'payable'>
00108|   >(
00109|     call: SimulateWriteCall<TAbi, TName>
00110|   ) => Promise<SimulateOneTransactionResult<TAbi, TName>>
00111|   simulateTwoTransactions: <
00112|     TAbi1 extends Abi,
00113|     TName1 extends ContractFunctionName<TAbi1, 'nonpayable' | 'payable'>,
00114|     TAbi2 extends Abi,
00115|     TName2 extends ContractFunctionName<TAbi2, 'nonpayable' | 'payable'>
00116|   >(
00117|     first: SimulateWriteCall<TAbi1, TName1>,
00118|     second: SimulateWriteCall<TAbi2, TName2>
00119|   ) => Promise<SimulateTwoTransactionsResult<TAbi1, TName1, TAbi2, TName2>>
00120|   error: Error | null
00121|   loading: boolean
00122| }
00123| 
00124| /**
00125|  * Simulate contract write calls before execution
00126|  * 
00127|  * @example
00128|  * ```typescript
00129|  * const { simulateOneTransaction, simulateTwoTransactions } = useSimulateWriteContracts()
00130|  * 
00131|  * // 1) Strongly typed single-call simulation
00132|  * const one = await simulateOneTransaction({
00133|  *   contractAddress: tokenAddress,
00134|  *   chainId: 8453,
00135|  *   abi: ERC20_ABI,
00136|  *   functionName: 'transfer' as const,
00137|  *   args: [recipient as Address, 1n] as [Address, bigint],
00138|  * })
00139|  * // one.callResult.result is typed to the return value of transfer
00140|  * if (one.callResult.status === 'success') {
00141|  *   const value = one.callResult.result // typed
00142|  * }
00143|  * 
00144|  * // 2) Strongly typed two-call simulation (e.g., approve then swap)
00145|  * const two = await simulateTwoTransactions(
00146|  *   {
00147|  *     contractAddress: tokenAddress,
00148|  *     chainId: 8453,
00149|  *     abi: ERC20_ABI,
00150|  *     functionName: 'approve' as const,
00151|  *     args: [spender as Address, 1n] as [Address, bigint],
00152|  *   },
00153|  *   {
00154|  *     contractAddress: swapManager,
00155|  *     chainId: 8453,
00156|  *     abi: SWAP_MANAGER_ABI,
00157|  *     functionName: 'swapExactTokenAmountForEth' as const,
00158|  *     args: [tokenAddress as Address, 1n, 0n] as [Address, bigint, bigint],
00159|  *   }
00160|  * )
00161|  * // two.firstCallResult.result is typed to the return value of approve; two.secondCallResult.result is typed to the return value of swap
00162|  * if (two.secondCallResult.status === 'success') {
00163|  *   const out = two.secondCallResult.result // typed
00164|  * }
00165|  * ```
00166|  */
00167| export function useSimulateWriteContracts(): UseSimulateWriteContractsReturn {
00168|   const { address: walletAddress } = useWallet()
00169|   const effectiveAddress = walletAddress
00170|   
00171|   const [error, setError] = useState<Error | null>(null)
00172|   const [loading, setLoading] = useState(false)
00173| 
00174|   /**
00175|    * Simulates a single transaction
00176|    */
00177|   const simulateSingleTransaction = useCallback(async <
00178|     TAbi extends Abi,
00179|     TName extends ContractFunctionName<TAbi, 'nonpayable' | 'payable'>
00180|   >(
00181|     txData: SimulateWriteCall<TAbi, TName>
00182|   ): Promise<CallResult<TAbi, TName>> => {
00183|     try {
00184|       const chain = getChainById(txData.chainId)
00185|       if (!chain) {
00186|         throw new Error(`Unsupported chain ID: ${txData.chainId}`)
00187|       }
00188| 
00189|       const publicClient = createPublicClient({
00190|         chain,
00191|         transport: http(getSimulationRpcUrl(txData.chainId)),
00192|       })
00193| 
00194|       // Note: We need to cast here due to viem's complex generic constraints
00195|       // The types are correct but TypeScript can't infer through the generic boundaries
00196|       const { result } = await publicClient.simulateContract({
00197|         abi: txData.abi,
00198|         address: txData.contractAddress,
00199|         functionName: txData.functionName,
00200|         args: txData.args,
00201|         value: txData.value,
00202|         account: effectiveAddress as Address,
00203|       } as any)
00204| 
00205|       return {
00206|         result: result as ContractFunctionReturnType<TAbi, 'nonpayable' | 'payable', TName>,
00207|         logs: [],
00208|         status: 'success' as const,
00209|         gasUsed: undefined,
00210|       }
00211|     } catch (err: any) {
00212|       return {
00213|         result: undefined,
00214|         logs: [],
00215|         status: 'failure' as const,
00216|         gasUsed: undefined,
00217|         error: {
00218|           message: err.message || 'Call failed',
00219|         },
00220|       }
00221|     }
00222|   }, [effectiveAddress])
00223| 
00224|   /**
00225|    * Simulates multiple transactions atomically
00226|    */
00227|   const simulateMultipleTransactionsAtomic = useCallback(async (
00228|     transactions: SimulateWriteCall[]
00229|   ): Promise<{ callResults: CallResult[]; assetChanges?: ReadonlyArray<AssetChange> }> => {
00230|     if (transactions.length === 0) {
00231|       return { callResults: [], assetChanges: undefined }
00232|     }
00233| 
00234|     // Verify all transactions are on the same chain
00235|     const chainId = transactions[0].chainId
00236|     if (!transactions.every(tx => tx.chainId === chainId)) {
00237|       throw new Error('All transactions must be on the same chain for atomic simulation')
00238|     }
00239| 
00240|     let chain = getChainById(chainId)
00241|     if (!chain) {
00242|       throw new Error(`Unsupported chain ID: ${chainId}`)
00243|     }
00244| 
00245|     const networkConfig = config.networks[chainId as keyof typeof config.networks]
00246|     if (networkConfig?.simulateV1RpcSupportedRpcUrl) {
00247|       chain = {
00248|         ...chain,
00249|         rpcUrls: {
00250|           default: { http: [networkConfig.simulateV1RpcSupportedRpcUrl] },
00251|         },
00252|       }
00253|     }
00254| 
00255|     const publicClient = createPublicClient({
00256|       chain,
00257|       transport: http(getSimulationRpcUrl(chainId)),
00258|     })
00259| 
00260|     try {
00261|       const callsWithABI = transactions.map((txData) => ({
00262|         to: txData.contractAddress,
00263|         data: encodeFunctionData({
00264|           abi: txData.abi,
00265|           functionName: txData.functionName,
00266|           args: txData.args,
00267|         }),
00268|         value: txData.value || undefined,
00269|         abi: txData.abi,
00270|         functionName: txData.functionName,
00271|       }))
00272| 
00273|       // Execute atomic simulation
00274|       // Note: simulateCalls is an RPC-specific method, not part of standard viem
00275|       const simulateResponse = await publicClient.simulateCalls({
00276|         calls: callsWithABI.map(call => ({ to: call.to, data: call.data, value: call.value })),
00277|         account: effectiveAddress as Address,
00278|         traceAssetChanges: true,
00279|       })
00280| 
00281|       if (!simulateResponse.results) {
00282|         throw new Error(`simulateCalls failed`)
00283|       }
00284| 
00285|       const callResults = Array.isArray(simulateResponse.results) 
00286|         ? simulateResponse.results.map((result, i: number) => ({
00287|             result: result.result || result.data 
00288|               ? decodeFunctionResult({ 
00289|                   abi: callsWithABI[i].abi, 
00290|                   data: result.data, 
00291|                   functionName: callsWithABI[i].functionName 
00292|                 }) 
00293|               : null,
00294|             logs: result.logs || [],
00295|             status: result.status,
00296|             gasUsed: result.gasUsed,
00297|             error: result.error ? {
00298|               message: result.error.message,
00299|             } : undefined,
00300|           })) 
00301|         : []
00302| 
00303|       return { callResults: callResults, assetChanges: simulateResponse.assetChanges }
00304|     } catch (err: any) {
00305|       throw new Error(`simulateCalls failed: ${err}`)
00306|     }
00307|   }, [effectiveAddress])
00308| 
00309|   /**
00310|    * Simulates multiple transactions sequentially
00311|    */
00312|   const simulateMultipleTransactionsSequential = useCallback(async (
00313|     transactions: SimulateWriteCall[]
00314|   ): Promise<CallResult[]> => {
00315|     const results: CallResult[] = []
00316|     
00317|     for (const txData of transactions) {
00318|       const result = await simulateSingleTransaction(txData)
00319|       results.push(result)
00320|     }
00321|     
00322|     return results
00323|   }, [simulateSingleTransaction])
00324| 
00325|   /**
00326|    * Simulates contract calls with automatic strategy selection
00327|    */
00328|   const simulateOneTransaction = useCallback(async <
00329|     TAbi extends Abi,
00330|     TName extends ContractFunctionName<TAbi, 'nonpayable' | 'payable'>
00331|   >(
00332|     call: SimulateWriteCall<TAbi, TName>
00333|   ): Promise<SimulateOneTransactionResult<TAbi, TName>> => {
00334|     const transactions = [call]
00335| 
00336|     setLoading(true)
00337|     setError(null)
00338| 
00339|     try {
00340|       const chainId = transactions[0].chainId
00341|       if (transactions.some(tx => tx.chainId !== chainId)) {
00342|         throw new Error('All simulated transactions in one call must be on the same chain')
00343|       }
00344| 
00345|       const chain = getChainById(chainId)
00346|       if (!chain) {
00347|         throw new Error(`Unsupported chain ID: ${transactions[0].chainId}`)
00348|       }
00349| 
00350|       const networkConfig = config.networks[chainId as keyof typeof config.networks]
00351| 
00352|       // Single transaction without simulateV1: use direct simulation
00353|       if (!networkConfig?.simulateV1RpcSupportedRpcUrl && transactions.length === 1) {
00354|         const result = await simulateSingleTransaction(transactions[0])
00355|         const simulationResultsData: SimulateOneTransactionResult<TAbi, TName> = {
00356|           callResult: result as any,
00357|           assetChanges: undefined,
00358|         }
00359|         setLoading(false)
00360|         return simulationResultsData
00361|       }
00362| 
00363|       try {
00364|         const atomic = await simulateMultipleTransactionsAtomic(transactions)
00365|         const simulationResultsData: SimulateOneTransactionResult<TAbi, TName> = {
00366|           callResult: atomic.callResults[0] as any,
00367|           assetChanges: atomic.assetChanges,
00368|         }
00369|         setLoading(false)
00370| 
00371|         return simulationResultsData
00372|       } catch (atomicError) {
00373|         console.warn('Atomic simulation failed, falling back to sequential:', atomicError)
00374|         // Fall back to sequential simulation
00375|         const callResults = await simulateMultipleTransactionsSequential(transactions)
00376|         const simulationResultsData: SimulateOneTransactionResult<TAbi, TName> = {
00377|           callResult: callResults[0] as any,
00378|           assetChanges: undefined,
00379|         }
00380|         setLoading(false)
00381|         return simulationResultsData
00382|       }
00383|     } catch (err) {
00384|       const error = err instanceof Error ? err : new Error(String(err))
00385|       setError(error)
00386|       console.error('Simulation failed:', error)
00387|       setLoading(false)
00388|       return {
00389|         callResult: undefined as any,
00390|         assetChanges: undefined,
00391|       }
00392|     }
00393|   }, [simulateSingleTransaction, simulateMultipleTransactionsAtomic, simulateMultipleTransactionsSequential])
00394| 
00395|   const simulateTwoTransactions = useCallback(async <
00396|     TAbi1 extends Abi,
00397|     TName1 extends ContractFunctionName<TAbi1, 'nonpayable' | 'payable'>,
00398|     TAbi2 extends Abi,
00399|     TName2 extends ContractFunctionName<TAbi2, 'nonpayable' | 'payable'>
00400|   >(
00401|     first: SimulateWriteCall<TAbi1, TName1>,
00402|     second: SimulateWriteCall<TAbi2, TName2>
00403|   ): Promise<SimulateTwoTransactionsResult<TAbi1, TName1, TAbi2, TName2>> => {
00404|     const transactions = [first, second]
00405| 
00406|     setLoading(true)
00407|     setError(null)
00408| 
00409|     try {
00410|       const chainId = first.chainId
00411|       if (second.chainId !== chainId) {
00412|         throw new Error('Both transactions must be on the same chain')
00413|       }
00414| 
00415|       const chain = getChainById(chainId)
00416|       if (!chain) {
00417|         throw new Error(`Unsupported chain ID: ${chainId}`)
00418|       }
00419| 
00420|       
00421| 
00422|       try {
00423|         const atomic = await simulateMultipleTransactionsAtomic(transactions)
00424|         const simulationResultsData: SimulateTwoTransactionsResult<TAbi1, TName1, TAbi2, TName2> = {
00425|           firstCallResult: atomic.callResults[0] as any,
00426|           secondCallResult: atomic.callResults[1] as any,
00427|           assetChanges: atomic.assetChanges,
00428|         }
00429|         setLoading(false)
00430|         return simulationResultsData
00431|       } catch (atomicError) {
00432|         console.warn('Atomic simulation failed, falling back to sequential:', atomicError)
00433|         const callResults = await simulateMultipleTransactionsSequential(transactions)
00434|         const simulationResultsData: SimulateTwoTransactionsResult<TAbi1, TName1, TAbi2, TName2> = {
00435|           firstCallResult: callResults[0] as any,
00436|           secondCallResult: callResults[1] as any,
00437|           assetChanges: undefined,
00438|         }
00439|         setLoading(false)
00440|         return simulationResultsData
00441|       }
00442|     } catch (err) {
00443|       const error = err instanceof Error ? err : new Error(String(err))
00444|       setError(error)
00445|       console.error('Simulation failed:', error)
00446|       setLoading(false)
00447|       throw error
00448|     }
00449|   }, [simulateMultipleTransactionsAtomic, simulateMultipleTransactionsSequential])
00450| 
00451|   return {
00452|     simulateOneTransaction,
00453|     simulateTwoTransactions,
00454|     error,
00455|     loading,
00456|   }
00457| }
</file>

metadata.preview:
import { useState, useCallback } from 'react'
import {
  type Abi,
  type Address,
  type ContractFunctionName,
  type ContractFunctionArgs,
  type ContractFunctionReturnType,
  type Log,
  createPublicClient,
  http,
  encodeFunctionData,
  decodeFunctionResult,
  } from 'viem'
import { useWallet } from './useWallet'
import { getChainById, getSimulationRpcUrl } from '@/lib/blockchain/chains'
import { config } from '../config'

/**
 * Represents an asset balance change during transaction simulation
 */

state.title: frontend/src/hooks/useSimulateWriteContracts.ts
